name: Claude 3 Haiku
tagline: Anthropic's fast, low-cost Claude 3 model with vision and a 200K context — now deprecated, retiring April 19, 2026.
metaDescription: Claude 3 Haiku (claude-3-haiku-20240307), released March 13, 2024 — Anthropic's fastest, cheapest Claude 3 tier with vision, a 200K context window, and $0.25/$…
overview: Claude 3 Haiku is the smallest, fastest, and most cost-effective model in Anthropic's original Claude 3 family, released on March 13, 2024 alongside Claude 3 Sonnet and the flagship Claude 3 Opus. Served via the API as `claude-3-haiku-20240307`, it was positioned as a near-instant model for high-volume, latency-sensitive work — Anthropic described it as processing roughly 21K tokens (about 30 pages) per second for prompts under 32K tokens.

Like the rest of the Claude 3 line, Claude 3 Haiku is multimodal: it accepts both text and images (photos, charts, graphs, and technical diagrams) and returns text. It ships with a 200K-token context window and a knowledge cutoff of August 2023. Pricing was set at $0.25 per million input tokens and $1.25 per million output tokens — Anthropic illustrated the value by noting Haiku could analyze about 400 Supreme Court cases or 2,500 images for roughly one US dollar.

Claude 3 Haiku is now deprecated. Anthropic has scheduled `claude-3-haiku-20240307` for retirement on April 19, 2026, and recommends migrating to Claude Haiku 4.5 (`claude-haiku-4-5`), which offers far higher intelligence, a larger 200K context with 64K max output, and broader feature support. It sits at the base of the Claude Haiku lineage that runs through Claude 3.5 Haiku to Claude Haiku 4.5.
strengths: Very low cost — $0.25 input / $1.25 output per million tokens at launch, the cheapest Claude 3 tier
Fast, near-instant responses suited to high-volume and latency-sensitive workloads
Multimodal: accepts text and images (charts, diagrams, photos) with text output
Large 200K-token context window for a budget-tier model
Available across the Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud Vertex AI
useCases: High-volume, cost-sensitive tasks like classification, tagging, and content moderation
Customer-support and chat assistants needing fast, cheap responses
Bulk summarization and extraction over large documents (within the 200K context)
Basic image understanding — reading charts, diagrams, and scanned documents
Lightweight coding help and simple data processing at scale

faq.a: It is deprecated. Anthropic has scheduled the model ID claude-3-haiku-20240307 for retirement on April 19, 2026, after which API calls to it will fail. Anthropic recommends migrating to Claude Haiku 4.5 (claude-haiku-4-5).

faq.q: How much did Claude 3 Haiku cost?
faq.a: At launch it was priced at $0.25 per million input tokens and $1.25 per million output tokens — a 1:5 input-to-output ratio and the cheapest tier in the Claude 3 family.

faq.q: What is Claude 3 Haiku's context window and knowledge cutoff?
faq.a: It has a 200K-token context window, a maximum output of 4,096 tokens, and a training knowledge cutoff of August 2023.
Yes. Like all Claude 3 models, Haiku is multimodal — it accepts text and images (photos, charts, graphs, and technical diagrams) and returns text.
